query: What is the name of the stadium shown in the image? Please provide a brief history of it.

input.query: BIG RED CORNELL stadium name and history

final_answer:
The stadium shown in the image is Schoellkopf Field, which is located on the campus of Cornell University in Ithaca, New York. It is home to the Cornell Big Red football, sprint football, and lacrosse teams.

Brief History:
Schoellkopf Field was opened in 1915 and originally named in honor of Henry Schoellkopf, a former Cornell football coach. The stadium is known for its historic Crescent a large, curved concrete stand that was added in 1924 and remains one of the most iconic structures in Ivy League athletics. Over the years, Schoellkopf has hosted numerous memorable sporting events and continues to serve as a central venue for Cornell athletics.
gt_answer: The stadium is Schoellkopf Field. It opened in 1915 and was named after coach Henry Schoellkopf.
Schoellkopf Field is Cornell's stadium, home to football and lacrosse. It was established in 1915.
The name of the stadium is Schoellkopf Field. Built in 1915, it's one of the oldest Ivy League stadiums and is known for the Crescent Stand built in 1924.
